note: known-good pivot: leading article stripped by normalize(), exact alias-map hit
known-good pivot: full character name, unambiguous exact hit
the existing graph_test.ts/resolve_test.ts REQUIRED pivot case, restated as a golden case

G19 FIXED (step 4a, S190): build_alias_table.py now folds victim-phrase entries (VICTIM_IN-derived, e.g. "robb stark's death") into the all-node index that build_chat_bundle.py's build_alias_map() consumes, so the phrase is now an exact key in web/data/alias-map.json. Was: fell to the fuzzy stage (matchType "fuzzy", not "exact") despite landing on the right node.

G2 FIXED (step 4b, S190): build_alias_table.py now generates deterministic plural variants (foods/objects/materials/texts/titles/concepts/religions node categories only — proper-noun categories like characters/houses/events/locations are excluded to avoid nonsense transforms) from every short name-shaped alias/name/slug entry at build time. 'lemon cake' (the food node's own slug-derived alias) generates the 'lemon cakes' variant, so this phrase is now an EXACT hit instead of a low-confidence fuzzy fallback (previously best score 0.55 against unrelated foods).

G10 FIXED (step 4c, S190): candidate-length penalty added to _fuzzy_candidates (resolve.py) / fuzzyCandidates (resolve.ts) — a candidate phrase LONGER than the query is discounted by min(1.0, |query|/|candidate|) before the slug-token bonus. Previously EVERY candidate whose phrase fully contained the single query token tied at score 1.0 (full overlap + slug-token bonus cap), so the whole-graph prominence tie-break alone decided, and tyrion-lannister (prominence 462) wrongly outranked tywin-lannister (prominence 218). Now: 2-token candidate phrases ('tywin lannister', 'death of tywin', ...) survive at a length-penalized 0.55 while tyrion-lannister's only matching phrase ('lord tywin's bane', 3 tokens) is penalized below the 0.5 floor and drops out of the candidate list entirely. Landed the OPPOSITE direction from the pre-fix pin (which expected tyrion-lannister to win) — this is the corrected behavior, not a regression.

G10 FIXED (step 4c, S190), full-profile companion to resolve-tywin-bare-name-debiased. The full-profile event_alias_resolver.resolve() has no exact key for 'house targaryen' (that phrase lives only in the all-node index, not the event-alias table its exact stage checks), so it falls to the fuzzy stage. Before step 4c: 'house targaryen' (2 tokens) tied at score 1.0 against the book-title node dragonkin-being-a-history-of-house-targaryen-... (13 tokens, query fully contained inside it), and the tie went to whichever the scan visited last / prominence — an unreliable pin. After step 4c's length penalty: the 13-token book-title candidate is discounted to ~0.15 (well below the 0.5 floor) and drops out entirely, leaving house-targaryen as the clear top candidate at 1.0. (The bounded/TS profile already resolved this phrase correctly via an exact alias-map hit — this case exercises the full-profile fuzzy path specifically, which is where the bias mechanism lived.)

step 4c design note ("beef vs assassination-of-tywin"): 'beef' is an exact alias-map hit on the food node (from its own node-slug alias), so it never reaches the fuzzy stage at all -- confirms the length-penalty / fuzzy-debias work does not disturb an unrelated exact hit that happens to share no meaningful token overlap with any Tywin-assassination-related phrase.
restates the existing resolve_test.ts case as a shared golden case

restates resolve_test.ts's prominence-tiebreak case: a shared phrase resolves to several exact slugs (both score 1.0); the content-rich node (16 quotes) must outrank the empty bare-name bucket (0 quotes).
